zone_6b_notes: Zone 6b is at the reliable northern edge for peach production â€” Farmington generally gets adequate crops. Late spring frosts are the main risk: peach blooms in early April, often before last frost. Plant on elevated ground or a north-facing slope to delay bloom and avoid frost damage. Contender and Reliance are the most cold-hardy varieties for Zone 6b and worth prioritizing. Peaches are self-fruitful â€” no pollinator required. Fruit thinning (remove small fruits 4â€“6 inches apart) is essential for large, quality fruit. Trees decline after 10â€“15 years and must be replanted. Prized for flavor and texture but requires considerable care; one mature tree produces 120-150 pounds of fruit annually.

planting.best_planting_time: Plant dormant bare-root trees March to early April

water: 1â€“1.5 inches/week; consistent moisture during fruit development
